response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, senior full-stack engineer with genuine systems experience (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second) and strong mentoring instincts, but he falls far short of the role's core requirement of 8 years of C++ — his documented C++ work amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project. His career centers on web and product engineering in TypeScript/Node.js rather than building cloud compute platforms or large-scale infrastructure, and he has no formal technical-leadership role leading platform or DevOps teams.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, caching)
Broad engineering range with strong testing culture (TDD, CI/CD, open-source test tooling author)
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing through workshops, courses, and community talks
concerns: C++ experience is under one year against the 8-year minimum requirement
No production cloud-platform or compute-infrastructure ownership; cloud exposure is limited to AWS CDK and Kubernetes deployment
No formal technical-lead role managing priorities, deliverables, or cross-team alignment
